configuration.prompt:
You are a professional release-notes writer for superplanehq/superplane.

NEW_VERSION = v0.27.0

Step 1 - Clone https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane and check out the commit from this release branch: c4adb7ab4cb4931038a09c017ad057283b3c2503.

Step 2 - Ensure the exact git-cliff configuration is available as `cliff.toml` in the cloned repository. If the checked-out commit does not contain `cliff.toml`, create it by decoding this base64 payload:



Step 3 - Study recent releases with `gh release list --limit 5 --repo superplanehq/superplane` and identify PREV_TAG from the latest prior release.

Step 4 - Run `git-cliff --config cliff.toml --tag "$NEW_VERSION" "$PREV_TAG..HEAD"` and use the generated grouped commits as raw material.

Step 5 - Replace the CUSTOM SUMMARY PLACEHOLDER section with a curated summary in the same house style as recent releases. Keep the generated grouped commit sections and the Full Changelog link.

Step 6 - Revise the previous changelog using the reviewer rejection reason.

Previous generated changelog:
## What's new since v0.26.0

v0.27.0 doubles down on the Git-first App workflow introduced in v0.26.0. The stage, commit, and publish loop is now noticeably smoother — duplicate draft requests are deduped, the commit button stays disabled until staging finishes, and stale draft fetches no longer fire after a version is deleted. A large round of dead canvas UI was removed along the way, which makes the editor lighter and easier to reason about.

This release also significantly expands the App catalog with six new prebuilt apps, adds Cloud Storage and EC2 load balancer support, polishes the Console dashboard, and tightens how Agents handle drafts, tokens, and integration resources.

### App install and catalog

The App install flow is more flexible and the catalog is much bigger. Install params now substitute cleanly into seed files, integration-resource params can use the resource name as the stored value, and the catalog now renders Lucide icons.

Six new prebuilt apps shipped to the manifest:

- **OSS Guard** — license and dependency policy guard for open source workflows.
- **TLS Monitor** — certificate expiration monitoring.
- **Semantic Pull Requests** — enforce conventional PR titles.
- **Cluster Autoscaler** — DigitalOcean + Grafana + Discord pipeline for cluster scaling.
- **PR Docs Review** — automated docs review on pull requests.
- **Auto Merge Bot** — policy-driven PR auto-merging.

The Coolify Watcher app was also added to the manifest, and the catalog was reordered by category.

### New components and integration expansion

Cloudsmith support grew substantially with `onPackageCreated` and `onSecurityScanCompleted` triggers and new `resyncPackage`, `tagPackage`, and `deletePackage` actions.

AWS EC2 gained `createLoadbalancer` and `deleteLoadBalancer` components, and `createInstance` was expanded with more launch options. GCP added a full set of Cloud Storage bucket components, and existing GCP component icons were refreshed.

GitHub triggers now cover all pull request webhook actions, removing the previous allow-list and unblocking less common PR event flows.

### Agents

Agents picked up two new building blocks for working with Apps:

- A `list_resources` action so agents can enumerate integration-backed resources before configuring nodes.
- Patch-style draft updates, which let agents apply targeted node and edge edits instead of resending whole canvas YAML.

Agent token usage tracking was tightened end to end: stable usage IDs, sync before publishing tokens, managed-agent session usage tracking, and non-blocking stream completion when usage writes are slow. Agent tool descriptions are now capped to stay within provider limits.

### Console and Canvas polish

Console dashboards got a visual pass — refreshed panels and a cleaner chart palette. The Table widget link field now supports CEL, the node widget panel got a round of fixes, and the canvas header stays above the runs sidebar and notes stay visible while inspecting runs. Memory variables are now available in autocomplete suggestions.

### Workflow execution fixes

A pile of execution edge cases were closed out: 5-field cron ranges now validate correctly, multiline runner setup commands are preserved, stale execution events no longer downgrade finished nodes, webhook-finalized executions publish their final state, `previous()` now resolves the latest upstream event, missing execution node references resolve as nil, and pending requests against deleted nodes are processed cleanly. Tasks now respect a configurable SuperPlane webhook payload size limit.

### Removals and cleanup

Core `sendEmail` notifications and the email invitation API were removed. A large amount of dead canvas plumbing was deleted: legacy YAML modal, unsaved-state and manual save UI, save activity log, changeset apply/validate endpoints, legacy view modes, and unused UI props and components. `RunSpan` was replaced with defer-style spans, and a new `grpcerror` package centralizes gRPC error handling.

### Bug Fixes

- Hide signup admin setting without waitlist config ([#5590](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5590)) by @forestileao
- Submit waitlist emails with native form ([#5591](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5591)) by @forestileao
- Update preview-env-digitalocean manifest entry ([#5601](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5601)) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Update preview-env-github-aws manifest entry ([#5602](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5602)) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Avoid DescribeCanvas requests in canvas edit mode ([#5629](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5629)) by @mchalapuk
- Remove core sendEmail notifications ([#5635](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5635)) by @forestileao
- Remove email invitation API ([#5638](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5638)) by @forestileao
- Add magic code email worker metrics ([#5637](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5637)) by @forestileao
- Keep canvas header above runs sidebar ([#5642](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5642)) by @forestileao
- Publish only clean drafts without auto-committing ([#5632](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5632)) by @lucaspin
- Only lock CanvasNodeRequest for processing when still pending ([#5622](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5622)) by @lucaspin
- Node Widget Improvements ([#5640](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5640)) by @andrecalil
- Simplify usage settings limits display ([#5654](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5654)) by @forestileao
- Publish agent token usage events ([#5655](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5655)) by @forestileao
- Preserve usage setup precondition errors ([#5661](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5661)) by @forestileao
- Portal rubric plan modal ([#5668](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5668)) by @forestileao
- Sync usage before publishing agent tokens ([#5669](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5669)) by @forestileao
- Track managed agent session usage ([#5671](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5671)) by @forestileao
- Avoid blocking agent stream completion on usage ([#5672](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5672)) by @forestileao
- Always refetch usage settings page ([#5673](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5673)) by @forestileao
- Ignore version creation invalidations on same tab ([#5680](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5680)) by @lucaspin
- Emit canvas_version_deleted and stop stale draft fetches after delete ([#5686](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5686)) by @lucaspin
- Suppress duplicate draft creation requests from versions sidebar ([#5690](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5690)) by @lucaspin
- Dedupe commit and publish draft staging API requests ([#5692](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5692)) by @lucaspin
- Keep commit button disabled until staging action fully completes ([#5693](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5693)) by @lucaspin
- Preserve multiline runner setup commands ([#5695](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5695)) by @forestileao
- Stop stale execution events downgrading finished nodes ([#5696](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5696)) by @forestileao
- Publish execution state after webhook-finalized executions ([#5697](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5697)) by @forestileao
- Keep notes visible while viewing canvas runs ([#5699](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5699)) by @shiroyasha
- Make previous resolve latest upstream event ([#5702](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5702)) by @forestileao
- Resolve missing execution node references as nil ([#5707](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5707)) by @forestileao
- Harden release image build prerequisites ([#5698](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5698)) by @forestileao
- Use ctx when listing integrations, service accounts, and invite link ([#5735](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5735)) by @lucaspin
- Publish stable agent token usage ids ([#5682](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5682)) by @forestileao
- Stop marking expected org auth failures as span errors ([#5764](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5764)) by @shiroyasha
- Validate 5-field cron ranges correctly ([#4825](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/4825)) by @immanuwell
- Process pending requests for deleted nodes ([#5765](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5765)) by @lucaspin
- Keep agent tool descriptions within provider limit ([#5769](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5769)) by @forestileao
- Table Link Not Supporting CEL ([#5768](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5768)) by @andrecalil

### Improvements

- Remove ExecutionChainPage component ([#5593](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5593)) by @lucaspin
- Metrics for webhook workers ([#5595](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5595)) by @lucaspin
- Use gauges for worker tick batch size metrics ([#5596](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5596)) by @lucaspin
- Add Coolify Watcher to manifest ([#5585](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5585)) by @andrecalil
- Update Readme ([#5636](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5636)) by @markoa
- Remove dead canvas save activity log plumbing ([#5643](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5643)) by @lucaspin

data.lastMessage:
## What's new since v0.26.0

v0.27.0 doubles down on the Git-first App workflow introduced in v0.26.0. The stage, commit, and publish loop is now noticeably smoother — duplicate draft requests are deduped, the commit button stays disabled until staging finishes, and stale draft fetches no longer fire after a version is deleted. A large round of dead canvas UI was removed along the way, which makes the editor lighter and easier to reason about.

This release also significantly expands the App catalog with six new prebuilt apps, adds Cloud Storage and EC2 load balancer support, polishes the Console dashboard, and tightens how Agents handle drafts, tokens, and integration resources.

### App install and catalog

The App install flow is more flexible and the catalog is much bigger. Install params now substitute cleanly into seed files, integration-resource params can use the resource name as the stored value, and the catalog now renders Lucide icons.

Six new prebuilt apps shipped to the manifest:

- **OSS Guard** — license and dependency policy guard for open source workflows.
- **TLS Monitor** — certificate expiration monitoring.
- **Semantic Pull Requests** — enforce conventional PR titles.
- **Cluster Autoscaler** — DigitalOcean + Grafana + Discord pipeline for cluster scaling.
- **PR Docs Review** — automated docs review on pull requests.
- **Auto Merge Bot** — policy-driven PR auto-merging.

The Coolify Watcher app was also added to the manifest, and the catalog was reordered by category.

### New components and integration expansion

Cloudsmith support grew substantially with `onPackageCreated` and `onSecurityScanCompleted` triggers and new `resyncPackage`, `tagPackage`, and `deletePackage` actions.

AWS EC2 gained `createLoadbalancer` and `deleteLoadBalancer` components, and `createInstance` was expanded with more launch options. GCP added a full set of Cloud Storage bucket components, and existing GCP component icons were refreshed.

GitHub triggers now cover all pull request webhook actions, removing the previous allow-list and unblocking less common PR event flows.

### Agents

Agents picked up two new building blocks for working with Apps:

- A `list_resources` action so agents can enumerate integration-backed resources before configuring nodes.
- Patch-style draft updates, which let agents apply targeted node and edge edits instead of resending whole canvas YAML.

Agent token usage tracking was tightened end to end: stable usage IDs, sync before publishing tokens, managed-agent session usage tracking, and non-blocking stream completion when usage writes are slow. Agent tool descriptions are now capped to stay within provider limits.

### Console and Canvas polish

Console dashboards got a visual pass — refreshed panels and a cleaner chart palette. The Table widget link field now supports CEL, the node widget panel got a round of fixes, and the canvas header stays above the runs sidebar and notes stay visible while inspecting runs. Memory variables are now available in autocomplete suggestions.

### Workflow execution fixes

A pile of execution edge cases were closed out: 5-field cron ranges now validate correctly, multiline runner setup commands are preserved, stale execution events no longer downgrade finished nodes, webhook-finalized executions publish their final state, `previous()` now resolves the latest upstream event, missing execution node references resolve as nil, and pending requests against deleted nodes are processed cleanly. Tasks now respect a configurable SuperPlane webhook payload size limit.

### Removals and cleanup

Core `sendEmail` notifications and the email invitation API were removed. A large amount of dead canvas plumbing was deleted: legacy YAML modal, unsaved-state and manual save UI, save activity log, changeset apply/validate endpoints, legacy view modes, and unused UI props and components. `RunSpan` was replaced with defer-style spans, and a new `grpcerror` package centralizes gRPC error handling.

- Submit waitlist emails with native form ([#5591](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5591)) by @forestileao
- Update preview-env-digitalocean manifest entry ([#5601](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5601)) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1

- Avoid DescribeCanvas requests in canvas edit mode ([#5629](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5629)) by @mchalapuk
- Remove core sendEmail notifications ([#5635](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5635)) by @forestileao
- Remove email invitation API ([#5638](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5638)) by @forestileao
- Add magic code email worker metrics ([#5637](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5637)) by @forestileao
- Keep canvas header above runs sidebar ([#5642](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5642)) by @forestileao
- Publish only clean drafts without auto-committing ([#5632](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5632)) by @lucaspin
- Only lock CanvasNodeRequest for processing when still pending ([#5622](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5622)) by @lucaspin
- Node Widget Improvements ([#5640](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5640)) by @andrecalil
- Simplify usage settings limits display ([#5654](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5654)) by @forestileao

configuration.body:
## What's new since v0.26.0

v0.27.0 is a catalog and integration release. The SuperPlane App catalog grows with seven new pre-built apps, Cloudsmith jumps from a base integration to a full one with new triggers and lifecycle actions, and AWS, GCP, and GitHub each pick up new components. It also lands a long list of polish fixes for the draft, commit, and publish flow introduced in v0.26.0, and quietly retires a lot of legacy canvas plumbing.

### App catalog and install experience

The catalog now ships with several new ready-to-install apps:

- OSS Guard
- TLS Monitor
- Semantic Pull Requests
- Coolify Watcher
- Cluster Autoscaler (DigitalOcean + Grafana + Discord)
- PR Docs Review
- Auto Merge Bot

The DigitalOcean and GitHub + AWS preview-environment manifests were refreshed, and the manifest itself was reordered by category for easier browsing.

The install wizard added in v0.26.0 is more capable too. Install params are now substituted into seed files when an app is created, integration-resource fields support a `useNameAsValue` option for providers that key on names rather than IDs, and the app catalog can render Lucide icons alongside the existing icon sets.

### Cloudsmith

Cloudsmith went from a base integration to a fully-featured one:

- New triggers: `onPackageCreated` and `onSecurityScanCompleted`.
- New actions: `resyncPackage`, `tagPackage`, and `deletePackage`, on top of the `getPackage` shipped in v0.26.0.

This makes it possible to drive package promotion, retagging, security follow-up, and cleanup directly from SuperPlane workflows.

### Other integration and component additions

- **AWS EC2** gained `createLoadBalancer` and `deleteLoadBalancer` components, and the existing `createInstance` action exposes more of EC2's launch options.
- **GCP Cloud Storage** is now first-class, with new bucket components, and existing GCP component icons were refreshed.
- **GitHub** webhook triggers now cover the full set of pull request actions, so workflows can react to every state change emitted by the GitHub webhook.
- Memory variables show up in expression autocomplete, making persisted state easier to discover and reference.
- Tasks accept a configurable **webhook payload size limit**, so larger inbound payloads can be wired through without hitting a hard cap.

### Agents

Two new agent tools tighten the edit and discovery loop:

- `patch-style draft updates` let agents apply small graph edits to an app draft without rewriting the whole canvas YAML.
- A new `list resources` action lets agents enumerate provider-backed integration resources (repositories, projects, workspaces, etc.) the same way the UI does.

Agent tool descriptions are now trimmed to fit Anthropic's provider-side limit, so registering many tools no longer fails the session.

### Console and node widgets

The Console dashboard panels and chart palette were polished as part of the ongoing Console rollout. Table Link cells now resolve CEL expressions, matching the rest of the Table widget, and a handful of node widget paint problems were ironed out.

### Drafts, canvas, and execution reliability

This release closes a long list of edge cases in the draft, commit, and publish flow introduced in v0.26.0:

- Publish now only triggers on clean drafts and no longer silently auto-commits staged changes.
- The versions sidebar dedupes draft creation and staging requests, and the commit button stays disabled until the staging action fully completes.
- Canvas edit mode no longer issues `DescribeCanvas` requests it does not need, and stale `canvas_version_*` invalidations from the same tab are ignored.
- A new `canvas_version_deleted` event stops stale draft fetches after a draft is removed, and notes stay visible while viewing canvas runs.
- `CanvasNodeRequest` rows are only locked when still pending, and pending requests for deleted nodes are processed instead of getting stuck.

On the execution side, expression handling, event ordering, and cron parsing all got safer:

- Missing expression tokens resolve to `null` instead of raising, and missing execution node references resolve to nil.
- `previous()` now resolves to the latest upstream event.
- Stale execution events can no longer downgrade a node that already finished, and webhook-finalized executions reliably publish their final state.
- 5-field cron expressions are validated with the correct field ranges, fixing a long-standing parser bug.
- Multiline runner setup commands round-trip without losing line breaks.

### Platform cleanup, toolchain, and observability

The frontend toolchain moved to Vite 8 and Storybook 10.4.6. On the backend, `RunSpan` was retired in favor of defer-style spans, a new `grpcerror` package centralizes gRPC error handling, and a long tail of dead canvas plumbing (changesets, view modes, save/unsaved-state UI, the legacy YAML modal, `ExecutionChainPage`) was removed.

Observability got a few quieter improvements: webhook workers and the magic code email worker emit dedicated metrics, expected org auth failures are no longer reported as span errors, Sentry CDN production builds emit sourcemaps, and Dash0 export noise is filtered out of Sentry.

### Release engineering

SuperPlane's own release pipeline now generates a changelog and tags releases automatically — the pipeline used to produce these notes.

### Bug Fixes

- Hide signup admin setting without waitlist config (#5590) by @forestileao
- Submit waitlist emails with native form (#5591) by @forestileao
- Update preview-env-digitalocean manifest entry (#5601) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Update preview-env-github-aws manifest entry (#5602) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Avoid DescribeCanvas requests in canvas edit mode (#5629) by @mchalapuk
- Remove core sendEmail notifications (#5635) by @forestileao
- Remove email invitation API (#5638) by @forestileao
- Add magic code email worker metrics (#5637) by @forestileao
- Keep canvas header above runs sidebar (#5642) by @forestileao
- Publish only clean drafts without auto-committing (#5632) by @lucaspin
- Only lock CanvasNodeRequest for processing when still pending (#5622) by @lucaspin
- Node Widget Improvements (#5640) by @andrecalil
- Simplify usage settings limits display (#5654) by @forestileao
- Publish agent token usage events (#5655) by @forestileao
- Preserve usage setup precondition errors (#5661) by @forestileao
- Portal rubric plan modal (#5668) by @forestileao
- Sync usage before publishing agent tokens (#5669) by @forestileao
- Track managed agent session usage (#5671) by @forestileao
- Avoid blocking agent stream completion on usage (#5672) by @forestileao
- Always refetch usage settings page (#5673) by @forestileao
- Ignore version creation invalidations on same tab (#5680) by @lucaspin
- Emit canvas_version_deleted and stop stale draft fetches after delete (#5686) by @lucaspin
- Suppress duplicate draft creation requests from versions sidebar (#5690) by @lucaspin
- Dedupe commit and publish draft staging API requests (#5692) by @lucaspin
- Keep commit button disabled until staging action fully completes (#5693) by @lucaspin
- Preserve multiline runner setup commands (#5695) by @forestileao
- Stop stale execution events downgrading finished nodes (#5696) by @forestileao
- Publish execution state after webhook-finalized executions (#5697) by @forestileao
- Keep notes visible while viewing canvas runs (#5699) by @shiroyasha
- Make previous resolve latest upstream event (#5702) by @forestileao
- Resolve missing execution node references as nil (#5707) by @forestileao
- Harden release image build prerequisites (#5698) by @forestileao
- Use ctx when listing integrations, service accounts, and invite link (#5735) by @lucaspin
- Publish stable agent token usage ids (#5682) by @forestileao
- Stop marking expected org auth failures as span errors (#5764) by @shiroyasha
- Validate 5-field cron ranges correctly (#4825) by @immanuwell
- Process pending requests for deleted nodes (#5765) by @lucaspin
- Keep agent tool descriptions within provider limit (#5769) by @forestileao
- Table Link Not Supporting CEL (#5768) by @andrecalil
- Emit sourcemaps on CDN production builds for Sentry (#5778) by @shiroyasha
- Silence Dash0 telemetry export noise in Sentry (#5784) by @shiroyasha
- Resolve missing expression tokens as null (#5786) by @forestileao

### Improvements

- Remove ExecutionChainPage component (#5593) by @lucaspin
- Metrics for webhook workers (#5595) by @lucaspin
- Use gauges for worker tick batch size metrics (#5596) by @lucaspin
- Add Coolify Watcher to manifest (#5585) by @andrecalil
- Update Readme (#5636) by @markoa
- Remove dead canvas save activity log plumbing (#5643) by @lucaspin
- Remove dead canvas unsaved-state and manual save UI (#5650) by @lucaspin
- Remove unused canvas changeset apply/validate endpoints (#5657) by @lucaspin
- Move CanvasChangeset from proto to internal changesets package (#5664) by @lucaspin
- Remove dead canvas UI plumbing and unused helpers (#5665) by @lucaspin
- Remove unused canvas UI props and dead components (#5666) by @lucaspin
- Remove legacy canvas view modes and stale header modes (#5667) by @lucaspin
- Introduce grpcerror package (#5677) by @shiroyasha
- Fix Models DB Linter to not be too sensitive (#5678) by @shiroyasha
- Remove legacy YAML modal (#5706) by @lucaspin
- Remove RunSpan in favor of defer style spans (#5767) by @shiroyasha
- Upgrade Storybook to 10.4.6 (#5776) by @shiroyasha
- Upgrade frontend toolchain to Vite 8 (#5777) by @shiroyasha

### New features

- Add cloudsmith onPackageCreated and onSecurityScanCompleted triggers (#5562) by @WashingtonKK
- App Installation Improvements (#5584) by @andrecalil
- Substitute install params in seed files (#5597) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add useNameAsValue for integration-resource install params (#5599) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add OSS Guard to app manifest (#5604) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add TLS Monitor to app manifest (#5605) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add Semantic Pull Requests to app manifest (#5606) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Support Lucide icons in app catalog (#5607) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add Cluster Autoscaler (DO + Grafana + Discord) to manifest (#5617) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add PR Docs Review to app manifest (#5618) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add Auto Merge Bot + reorder manifest by category (#5619) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add Cloudsmith resyncPackage, tagPackage and deletePackage components (#5568) by @felixgateru
- Add AWS EC2 createLoadbalancer and deleteLoadBalancer components (#5177) by @felixgateru
- Include memory to autocomplete (#5626) by @re-pixel
- Add GCP Cloud Storage bucket components (#5641) by @WashingtonKK
- Support all GitHub pull request webhook actions (#5687) by @shiroyasha
- Add release changelog and tag pipeline (#5694) by @forestileao
- Update gcp components icons (#5621) by @WashingtonKK
- Add the SuperPlane webhook payload size limit to tasks (#5701) by @re-pixel

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

WORKDIR=$(mktemp -d)
GIT_ASKPASS="$WORKDIR/git-askpass.sh"
cat > "$GIT_ASKPASS" <<'ASKPASS'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
case "$1" in
  Username*) printf '%s' 'x-access-token' ;;
  Password*) printf '%s' "$GITHUB_TOKEN" ;;
esac
ASKPASS
chmod 700 "$GIT_ASKPASS"
GIT_ASKPASS="$GIT_ASKPASS" git clone "https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane.git" "$WORKDIR/src"
cd "$WORKDIR/src"
git checkout "$COMMIT_SHA"

release/superplane-single-host-tarball/build.sh "$REL_VERSION"

TARBALL="build/superplane-single-host-tarball-${REL_VERSION}/superplane-single-host.tar.gz"
if [ ! -f "$TARBALL" ]; then
  echo "Tarball not produced: $TARBALL" >&2
  exit 1
fi

S3_KEY="tarball/${COMMIT_SHA}/superplane-single-host-${REL_VERSION}.tar.gz"
aws s3 cp "$TARBALL" "s3://${STAGING_BUCKET}/${S3_KEY}"

cat > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE" <<JSON
{"s3Uri":"s3://${STAGING_BUCKET}/${S3_KEY}","bucket":"$STAGING_BUCKET","key":"$S3_KEY","commit":"$COMMIT_SHA","version":"$REL_VERSION"}
JSON
configuration.setup_commands:
command -v aws >/dev/null 2>&1 || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl unzip && curl -sSL "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-x86_64.zip" -o /tmp/awscli.zip && unzip -q /tmp/awscli.zip -d /tmp && /tmp/aws/install)
command -v make >/dev/null 2>&1 || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential)

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
exec > >(tee /var/log/install.log|logger -t user-data -s 2>/dev/console) 2>&1

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get update
apt-get install -y curl docker.io openssl unzip
systemctl enable --now docker

mkdir -p /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins
curl -fsSL "https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/download/v2.40.3/docker-compose-linux-x86_64" \
  -o /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose
chmod +x /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose
docker compose version >/dev/null

curl -sSL "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-x86_64.zip" -o /tmp/awscli.zip
unzip -q /tmp/awscli.zip -d /tmp && /tmp/aws/install

aws ecr get-login-password --region us-east-1 \
  | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin 768979069867.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com

TARBALL_S3_URI='s3://superplane-release-staging-768979069867-us-east-1/tarball/a20345a6cff747f29620e0475385a56074a875b6/superplane-single-host-v0.27.0.tar.gz'
IMAGE='768979069867.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/superplane:a20345a6cff747f29620e0475385a56074a875b6'

aws s3 cp "$TARBALL_S3_URI" /tmp/sp.tgz
mkdir -p /opt/superplane
tar -xzf /tmp/sp.tgz -C /opt/superplane
cd /opt/superplane/superplane
sed -i -E "s|image: ghcr.io/superplanehq/superplane:[^[:space:]]+|image: ${IMAGE}|" docker-compose.yml

METADATA_TOKEN=$(curl -fsS -X PUT "http://169.254.169.254/latest/api/token" -H "X-aws-ec2-metadata-token-ttl-seconds: 21600")
PUBLIC_HOSTNAME=$(curl -fsS -H "X-aws-ec2-metadata-token: ${METADATA_TOKEN}" http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/public-hostname)
BASE_URL="http://${PUBLIC_HOSTNAME}"

ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 16)
JWT_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
SESSION_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
DB_PASSWORD=$(openssl rand -hex 16)
mkdir -p oidc
openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:4096 -out "oidc/$(date -u +%s).pem"
chmod 644 oidc/*.pem

cat > superplane.env <<ENVFILE
APP_ENV=production
APPLICATION_NAME=superplane
BASE_URL=${BASE_URL}
WEBHOOKS_BASE_URL=${BASE_URL}
SUPERPLANE_DOMAIN=:80
PUBLIC_API_BASE_PATH=/api/v1
WEB_BASE_PATH=
DB_HOST=db
DB_PORT=5432
DB_NAME=superplane
DB_USERNAME=postgres
DB_PASSWORD=${DB_PASSWORD}
POSTGRES_DB_SSL=false
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${DB_PASSWORD}
RABBITMQ_URL=amqp://guest:guest@rabbitmq:5672
GIT_STORAGE_PROVIDER=supergit
GIT_STORAGE_SUPERGIT_BASE_URL=http://supergit:8080/api
SWAGGER_BASE_PATH=/app/api/swagger
RBAC_MODEL_PATH=/app/rbac/rbac_model.conf
RBAC_ORG_POLICY_PATH=/app/rbac/rbac_org_policy.csv
TEMPLATE_DIR=/app/templates
ENCRYPTION_KEY=${ENCRYPTION_KEY}
JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}
OIDC_KEYS_PATH=/app/oidc-keys
SESSION_SECRET=${SESSION_SECRET}
NO_ENCRYPTION=no
OWNER_SETUP_ENABLED=yes
BLOCK_SIGNUP=yes
ENABLE_PASSWORD_LOGIN=yes
START_PUBLIC_API=yes
START_CONSUMERS=yes
START_WEB_SERVER=yes
START_EVENT_DISTRIBUTER=yes
START_EVENT_ROUTER=yes
START_RUN_FINALIZER=yes
START_NODE_EXECUTOR=yes
START_NODE_QUEUE_WORKER=yes
START_NODE_REQUEST_WORKER=yes
START_INTEGRATION_REQUEST_WORKER=yes
START_WEBHOOK_PROVISIONER=yes
START_WEBHOOK_CLEANUP_WORKER=yes
START_INTEGRATION_CLEANUP_WORKER=yes
START_CANVAS_CLEANUP_WORKER=yes
START_REPOSITORY_PROVISIONER=yes
SENTRY_DSN=
SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT=canary-tarball
SUPERPLANE_BEACON_ENABLED=no
SUPERPLANE_INSTALLATION_TYPE=canary-tarball
OTEL_ENABLED=no
OIDC_KEYS_HOST_PATH=${PWD}/oidc
OIDC_KEYS_CONTAINER_PATH=/app/oidc-keys
ENVFILE

cat > Caddyfile <<'CADDYFILE'
:80 {
  reverse_proxy app:8000
}
CADDYFILE

configuration.content: 🚨🚨🚨 NPM Publish Failed for v0.27.0 — check the pipeline for details

data.content: 🚨🚨🚨 NPM Publish Failed for v0.27.0 — check the pipeline for details

data.description: The open source control plane for agentic engineering.

repository.description: The open source control plane for agentic engineering.
repository.name: superplane

configuration.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CHECKS_FILE="$(mktemp)"
COOKIE_JAR="$(mktemp)"
printf '[]' > "$CHECKS_FILE"
FAILED=0
ORG_ID=""
CANVAS_ID=""
CANVAS_NAME=""

record_check() {
  local name="$1"
  local ok="$2"
  local duration_ms="$3"
  local details="${4:-{}}"

  if ! printf '%s' "$details" | jq -e . >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    details="{}"
  fi

  jq     --arg name "$name"     --arg ok "$ok"     --arg duration_ms "$duration_ms"     --arg details "$details"     'def parsed_details: try ($details | fromjson) catch {};
     . += [({name: $name, ok: ($ok == "true"), skipped: false, duration_ms: ($duration_ms | tonumber? // 0)} + parsed_details)]'     "$CHECKS_FILE" > "$CHECKS_FILE.tmp"
  mv "$CHECKS_FILE.tmp" "$CHECKS_FILE"

  if [ "$ok" = "true" ]; then
    echo "  ${name}: OK"
  else
    echo "  ${name}: FAILED"
    FAILED=1
  fi
}

now_ms() {
  date +%s%3N
}

duration_since() {
  local start="$1"
  echo "$(( $(now_ms) - start ))"
}

curl_status() {
  local output_file="$1"
  shift
  local status
  status="$(curl -sS -o "$output_file" -w "%{http_code}" "$@" || printf '000')"
  printf '%s' "$status"
}

echo "Smoke testing ${TARGET} at ${BASE_URL} (expected release: ${REL_VERSION})"

start="$(now_ms)"
health_ok=false
for attempt in $(seq 1 10); do
  if curl -fsS "$BASE_URL/health" >/dev/null; then
    health_ok=true
    break
  fi
  echo "  health-ready: attempt ${attempt} failed; retrying"
  sleep 3
done
if [ "$health_ok" = true ]; then
  record_check "health-ready" true "$(duration_since "$start")"
else
  record_check "health-ready" false "$(duration_since "$start")"
fi

start="$(now_ms)"
setup_payload="$(jq -n \
  --arg email "$OWNER_EMAIL" \
  --arg first_name "$OWNER_FIRST_NAME" \
  --arg last_name "$OWNER_LAST_NAME" \
  --arg password "$OWNER_PASSWORD" \
  '{email: $email, first_name: $first_name, last_name: $last_name, password: $password, smtp_enabled: false, allow_private_network_access: true}')"
setup_response="$(mktemp)"
setup_status="$(curl_status "$setup_response" \
  -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -X POST \
  --data "$setup_payload" \
  "$BASE_URL/api/v1/setup-owner")"

if [ "$setup_status" = "200" ]; then
  ORG_ID="$(jq -r '.organization_id // empty' "$setup_response")"
  if [ -n "$ORG_ID" ]; then
    record_check "owner-setup" true "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg organization_id "$ORG_ID" --arg status "$setup_status" '{organization_id: $organization_id, status: $status}')"
  else
    record_check "owner-setup" false "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg status "$setup_status" '{status: $status, error: "missing organization_id"}')"
  fi
elif [ "$setup_status" = "409" ] || [ "$setup_status" = "404" ]; then
  record_check "owner-setup" true "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg status "$setup_status" '{status: $status, idempotent: true}')"
else
  record_check "owner-setup" false "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg status "$setup_status" --rawfile body "$setup_response" '{status: $status, body: $body}')"
fi

start="$(now_ms)"
login_response="$(mktemp)"
login_status="$(curl_status "$login_response" \
  -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -X POST \
  --data-urlencode "email=$OWNER_EMAIL" \
  --data-urlencode "password=$OWNER_PASSWORD" \
  "$BASE_URL/login?redirect=%2F")"
case "$login_status" in
  200|302|303)
    record_check "owner-login" true "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg status "$login_status" '{status: $status}')"
    ;;
  *)
    record_check "owner-login" false "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg status "$login_status" --rawfile body "$login_response" '{status: $status, body: $body}')"
    ;;
esac

start="$(now_ms)"
organizations_response="$(mktemp)"
organizations_status="$(curl_status "$organizations_response" \
  -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  "$BASE_URL/organizations")"
if [ -z "$ORG_ID" ] && [ "$organizations_status" = "200" ]; then
  ORG_ID="$(jq -r '.[0].id // empty' "$organizations_response")"
fi
if [ "$organizations_status" = "200" ] && [ -n "$ORG_ID" ]; then
  record_check "resolve-organization" true "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg organization_id "$ORG_ID" '{organization_id: $organization_id}')"
else
  record_check "resolve-organization" false "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg status "$organizations_status" --rawfile body "$organizations_response" '{status: $status, body: $body}')"
fi

start="$(now_ms)"
me_response="$(mktemp)"
me_status="$(curl_status "$me_response" \
  -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -H "x-organization-id: $ORG_ID" \
  "$BASE_URL/api/v1/me")"
me_email="$(jq -r '.user.email // empty' "$me_response" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [ "$me_status" = "200" ] && [ "$me_email" = "$OWNER_EMAIL" ]; then
  record_check "authenticated-me" true "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg email "$me_email" '{email: $email}')"
else
  record_check "authenticated-me" false "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg status "$me_status" --arg email "$me_email" --rawfile body "$me_response" '{status: $status, email: $email, body: $body}')"
fi

start="$(now_ms)"
CANVAS_NAME="canary-${TARGET}-$(date -u +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)-${RANDOM}"
create_payload="$(jq -n \
  --arg name "$CANVAS_NAME" \
  --arg description "Release canary smoke test for ${TARGET}" \
  '{canvas: {metadata: {name: $name, description: $description}, spec: {nodes: [], edges: []}}}')"
create_response="$(mktemp)"
create_status="$(curl_status "$create_response" \
  -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "x-organization-id: $ORG_ID" \
  -X POST \
  --data "$create_payload" \
  "$BASE_URL/api/v1/canvases")"
CANVAS_ID="$(jq -r '.canvas.metadata.id // empty' "$create_response" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [ "$create_status" = "200" ] && [ -n "$CANVAS_ID" ]; then
  record_check "create-canvas" true "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg canvas_id "$CANVAS_ID" --arg name "$CANVAS_NAME" '{canvas_id: $canvas_id, name: $name}')"
else
  record_check "create-canvas" false "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg status "$create_status" --rawfile body "$create_response" '{status: $status, body: $body}')"
fi

start="$(now_ms)"
describe_response="$(mktemp)"
describe_status="$(curl_status "$describe_response" \
  -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -H "x-organization-id: $ORG_ID" \
  "$BASE_URL/api/v1/canvases/$CANVAS_ID")"
described_id="$(jq -r '.canvas.metadata.id // empty' "$describe_response" 2>/dev/null || true)"
described_name="$(jq -r '.canvas.metadata.name // empty' "$describe_response" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [ "$describe_status" = "200" ] && [ "$described_id" = "$CANVAS_ID" ] && [ "$described_name" = "$CANVAS_NAME" ]; then
  record_check "describe-canvas" true "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg canvas_id "$described_id" '{canvas_id: $canvas_id}')"
else
  record_check "describe-canvas" false "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg status "$describe_status" --arg canvas_id "$described_id" --arg name "$described_name" --rawfile body "$describe_response" '{status: $status, canvas_id: $canvas_id, name: $name, body: $body}')"
fi

start="$(now_ms)"
list_response="$(mktemp)"
list_status="$(curl_status "$list_response" \
  -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
  -H "x-organization-id: $ORG_ID" \
  "$BASE_URL/api/v1/canvases")"
listed="$(jq --arg id "$CANVAS_ID" '[.canvases[]?.id] | index($id) != null' "$list_response" 2>/dev/null || printf 'false')"
if [ "$list_status" = "200" ] && [ "$listed" = "true" ]; then
  record_check "list-canvases" true "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg canvas_id "$CANVAS_ID" '{canvas_id: $canvas_id}')"
else
  record_check "list-canvases" false "$(duration_since "$start")" "$(jq -n --arg status "$list_status" --arg listed "$listed" --rawfile body "$list_response" '{status: $status, listed: $listed, body: $body}')"
fi

passed=false
if [ "$FAILED" -eq 0 ]; then
  passed=true
fi

jq -n \
  --arg target "$TARGET" \
  --arg base "$BASE_URL" \
  --arg version "$REL_VERSION" \
  --arg owner "$OWNER_EMAIL" \
  --arg organization_id "$ORG_ID" \
  --arg canvas_id "$CANVAS_ID" \
  --arg passed "$passed" \
  --slurpfile checks "$CHECKS_FILE" \
  '{target: $target, passed: ($passed == "true"), base: $base, version: $version, owner: $owner, organization_id: $organization_id, canvas_id: $canvas_id, checks: $checks[0]}' \
  > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE"

if [ "$FAILED" -ne 0 ]; then
  exit 1
fi
configuration.setup_commands: command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends jq)

configuration.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

START_SECONDS=$(date +%s)

echo "Waiting for ${TARGET} readiness at ${BASE_URL}/health"
echo "Max attempts: ${MAX_ATTEMPTS}; sleep seconds: ${SLEEP_SECONDS}"

for attempt in $(seq 1 "$MAX_ATTEMPTS"); do
  if curl -fsS --connect-timeout 3 --max-time 5 "$BASE_URL/health" >/dev/null; then
    elapsed=$(( $(date +%s) - START_SECONDS ))
    echo "${TARGET} ready on attempt ${attempt}/${MAX_ATTEMPTS} after ${elapsed}s"
    cat > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE" <<JSON
{"target":"$TARGET","ready":true,"base":"$BASE_URL","attempts":$attempt,"max_attempts":$MAX_ATTEMPTS,"elapsed_seconds":$elapsed}
JSON
    exit 0
  fi

  elapsed=$(( $(date +%s) - START_SECONDS ))
  echo "${TARGET} not ready on attempt ${attempt}/${MAX_ATTEMPTS} after ${elapsed}s"

  if [ "$attempt" -lt "$MAX_ATTEMPTS" ]; then
    sleep "$SLEEP_SECONDS"
  fi
done

elapsed=$(( $(date +%s) - START_SECONDS ))
cat > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE" <<JSON
{"target":"$TARGET","ready":false,"base":"$BASE_URL","attempts":$MAX_ATTEMPTS,"max_attempts":$MAX_ATTEMPTS,"elapsed_seconds":$elapsed}
JSON

echo "Timed out waiting for ${TARGET} readiness after ${MAX_ATTEMPTS} attempts and ${elapsed}s" >&2
exit 1

configuration.prompt:
## OUTPUT FORMAT — read this first and obey it strictly

Your ENTIRE response must be ONLY the final `release.md` Markdown content.

The FIRST character of your response must be the first character of the release.md Markdown.
The LAST character of your response must be the last character of the release.md Markdown.
Nothing before. Nothing after. No exceptions.

DO NOT include any of the following anywhere in your response:
- Preamble such as "Now I have everything I need", "Let me write the changelog", "Here is the changelog", "I'll create...", or any opening narration.
- Closing remarks such as "Let me know if you need changes", "I hope this helps", "Done.", or sign-offs.
- Tool-use commentary, plan summaries, step recaps, or status updates about what you are about to do or what you just did.
- Any mention of token usage, cost, runtime, time spent, tracking, telemetry, statistics, or metadata about this generation.
- Code fences (```), JSON wrappers, XML tags, or any other framing around the Markdown.
- Any reasoning, explanation, or self-commentary about your output.

If you find yourself about to write anything that is not part of the changelog Markdown body, stop and discard it. Begin again with the changelog content.

---

You are a professional release-notes writer for superplanehq/superplane.

NEW_VERSION = v0.27.0

data.lastMessage:
## What's new since v0.26.0

v0.27.0 is a catalog and integration release. The SuperPlane App catalog grows with seven new pre-built apps, Cloudsmith jumps from a base integration to a full one with new triggers and lifecycle actions, and AWS, GCP, and GitHub each pick up new components. It also lands a long list of polish fixes for the draft, commit, and publish flow introduced in v0.26.0, and quietly retires a lot of legacy canvas plumbing.

### App catalog and install experience

The catalog now ships with several new ready-to-install apps:

- OSS Guard
- TLS Monitor
- Semantic Pull Requests
- Coolify Watcher
- Cluster Autoscaler (DigitalOcean + Grafana + Discord)
- PR Docs Review
- Auto Merge Bot

The DigitalOcean and GitHub + AWS preview-environment manifests were refreshed, and the manifest itself was reordered by category for easier browsing.

The install wizard added in v0.26.0 is more capable too. Install params are now substituted into seed files when an app is created, integration-resource fields support a `useNameAsValue` option for providers that key on names rather than IDs, and the app catalog can render Lucide icons alongside the existing icon sets.

### Cloudsmith

Cloudsmith went from a base integration to a fully-featured one:

- New triggers: `onPackageCreated` and `onSecurityScanCompleted`.
- New actions: `resyncPackage`, `tagPackage`, and `deletePackage`, on top of the `getPackage` shipped in v0.26.0.

This makes it possible to drive package promotion, retagging, security follow-up, and cleanup directly from SuperPlane workflows.

### Other integration and component additions

- **AWS EC2** gained `createLoadBalancer` and `deleteLoadBalancer` components, and the existing `createInstance` action exposes more of EC2's launch options.
- **GCP Cloud Storage** is now first-class, with new bucket components, and existing GCP component icons were refreshed.
- **GitHub** webhook triggers now cover the full set of pull request actions, so workflows can react to every state change emitted by the GitHub webhook.
- Memory variables show up in expression autocomplete, making persisted state easier to discover and reference.
- Tasks accept a configurable **webhook payload size limit**, so larger inbound payloads can be wired through without hitting a hard cap.

### Agents

Two new agent tools tighten the edit and discovery loop:

- `patch-style draft updates` let agents apply small graph edits to an app draft without rewriting the whole canvas YAML.
- A new `list resources` action lets agents enumerate provider-backed integration resources (repositories, projects, workspaces, etc.) the same way the UI does.

Agent tool descriptions are now trimmed to fit Anthropic's provider-side limit, so registering many tools no longer fails the session.

### Console and node widgets

The Console dashboard panels and chart palette were polished as part of the ongoing Console rollout. Table Link cells now resolve CEL expressions, matching the rest of the Table widget, and a handful of node widget paint problems were ironed out.

### Drafts, canvas, and execution reliability

This release closes a long list of edge cases in the draft, commit, and publish flow introduced in v0.26.0:

- Publish now only triggers on clean drafts and no longer silently auto-commits staged changes.
- The versions sidebar dedupes draft creation and staging requests, and the commit button stays disabled until the staging action fully completes.
- Canvas edit mode no longer issues `DescribeCanvas` requests it does not need, and stale `canvas_version_*` invalidations from the same tab are ignored.
- A new `canvas_version_deleted` event stops stale draft fetches after a draft is removed, and notes stay visible while viewing canvas runs.
- `CanvasNodeRequest` rows are only locked when still pending, and pending requests for deleted nodes are processed instead of getting stuck.

On the execution side, expression handling, event ordering, and cron parsing all got safer:

- Missing expression tokens resolve to `null` instead of raising, and missing execution node references resolve to nil.
- `previous()` now resolves to the latest upstream event.
- Stale execution events can no longer downgrade a node that already finished, and webhook-finalized executions reliably publish their final state.
- 5-field cron expressions are validated with the correct field ranges, fixing a long-standing parser bug.
- Multiline runner setup commands round-trip without losing line breaks.

### Platform cleanup, toolchain, and observability

The frontend toolchain moved to Vite 8 and Storybook 10.4.6. On the backend, `RunSpan` was retired in favor of defer-style spans, a new `grpcerror` package centralizes gRPC error handling, and a long tail of dead canvas plumbing (changesets, view modes, save/unsaved-state UI, the legacy YAML modal, `ExecutionChainPage`) was removed.

Observability got a few quieter improvements: webhook workers and the magic code email worker emit dedicated metrics, expected org auth failures are no longer reported as span errors, Sentry CDN production builds emit sourcemaps, and Dash0 export noise is filtered out of Sentry.

### Release engineering

SuperPlane's own release pipeline now generates a changelog and tags releases automatically — the pipeline used to produce these notes.

### Bug Fixes

- Hide signup admin setting without waitlist config (#5590) by @forestileao
- Submit waitlist emails with native form (#5591) by @forestileao
- Update preview-env-digitalocean manifest entry (#5601) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Update preview-env-github-aws manifest entry (#5602) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Avoid DescribeCanvas requests in canvas edit mode (#5629) by @mchalapuk
- Remove core sendEmail notifications (#5635) by @forestileao
- Remove email invitation API (#5638) by @forestileao
- Add magic code email worker metrics (#5637) by @forestileao
- Keep canvas header above runs sidebar (#5642) by @forestileao
- Publish only clean drafts without auto-committing (#5632) by @lucaspin

- Node Widget Improvements (#5640) by @andrecalil

- Sync usage before publishing agent tokens (#5669) by @forestileao
- Track managed agent session usage (#5671) by @forestileao

- Ignore version creation invalidations on same tab (#5680) by @lucaspin
- Emit canvas_version_deleted and stop stale draft fetches after delete (#5686) by @lucaspin
- Suppress duplicate draft creation requests from versions sidebar (#5690) by @lucaspin
- Dedupe commit and publish draft staging API requests (#5692) by @lucaspin
- Keep commit button disabled until staging action fully completes (#5693) by @lucaspin
- Preserve multiline runner setup commands (#5695) by @forestileao
- Stop stale execution events downgrading finished nodes (#5696) by @forestileao
- Publish execution state after webhook-finalized executions (#5697) by @forestileao
- Keep notes visible while viewing canvas runs (#5699) by @shiroyasha
- Make previous resolve latest upstream event (#5702) by @forestileao
- Resolve missing execution node references as nil (#5707) by @forestileao
- Harden release image build prerequisites (#5698) by @forestileao
- Use ctx when listing integrations, service accounts, and invite link (#5735) by @lucaspin
- Publish stable agent token usage ids (#5682) by @forestileao
- Stop marking expected org auth failures as span errors (#5764) by @shiroyasha
- Validate 5-field cron ranges correctly (#4825) by @immanuwell
- Process pending requests for deleted nodes (#5765) by @lucaspin
- Keep agent tool descriptions within provider limit (#5769) by @forestileao
- Table Link Not Supporting CEL (#5768) by @andrecalil
- Emit sourcemaps on CDN production builds for Sentry (#5778) by @shiroyasha
- Silence Dash0 telemetry export noise in Sentry (#5784) by @shiroyasha
- Resolve missing expression tokens as null (#5786) by @forestileao

### Improvements

- Remove ExecutionChainPage component (#5593) by @lucaspin
- Metrics for webhook workers (#5595) by @lucaspin
- Use gauges for worker tick batch size metrics (#5596) by @lucaspin
- Add Coolify Watcher to manifest (#5585) by @andrecalil
- Update Readme (#5636) by @markoa
- Remove dead canvas save activity log plumbing (#5643) by @lucaspin
- Remove dead canvas unsaved-state and manual save UI (#5650) by @lucaspin
- Remove unused canvas changeset apply/validate endpoints (#5657) by @lucaspin
- Move CanvasChangeset from proto to internal changesets package (#5664) by @lucaspin
- Remove dead canvas UI plumbing and unused helpers (#5665) by @lucaspin
- Remove unused canvas UI props and dead components (#5666) by @lucaspin
- Remove legacy canvas view modes and stale header modes (#5667) by @lucaspin
- Introduce grpcerror package (#5677) by @shiroyasha
- Fix Models DB Linter to not be too sensitive (#5678) by @shiroyasha
- Remove legacy YAML modal (#5706) by @lucaspin
- Remove RunSpan in favor of defer style spans (#5767) by @shiroyasha
- Upgrade Storybook to 10.4.6 (#5776) by @shiroyasha
- Upgrade frontend toolchain to Vite 8 (#5777) by @shiroyasha

### New features

- Add cloudsmith onPackageCreated and onSecurityScanCompleted triggers (#5562) by @WashingtonKK
- App Installation Improvements (#5584) by @andrecalil
- Substitute install params in seed files (#5597) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add useNameAsValue for integration-resource install params (#5599) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add OSS Guard to app manifest (#5604) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add TLS Monitor to app manifest (#5605) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add Semantic Pull Requests to app manifest (#5606) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Support Lucide icons in app catalog (#5607) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add Cluster Autoscaler (DO + Grafana + Discord) to manifest (#5617) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add PR Docs Review to app manifest (#5618) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add Auto Merge Bot + reorder manifest by category (#5619) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Add Cloudsmith resyncPackage, tagPackage and deletePackage components (#5568) by @felixgateru
- Add AWS EC2 createLoadbalancer and deleteLoadBalancer components (#5177) by @felixgateru
- Include memory to autocomplete (#5626) by @re-pixel
- Add GCP Cloud Storage bucket components (#5641) by @WashingtonKK
- Support all GitHub pull request webhook actions (#5687) by @shiroyasha
- Add release changelog and tag pipeline (#5694) by @forestileao
- Update gcp components icons (#5621) by @WashingtonKK
- Add the SuperPlane webhook payload size limit to tasks (#5701) by @re-pixel

- Polish console dashboard panels and chart palette (#5766) by @ropsii

valueList.name: changelog
valueList.value:
## What's new since v0.26.0

v0.27.0 doubles down on the Git-first App workflow introduced in v0.26.0. The stage, commit, and publish loop is now noticeably smoother — duplicate draft requests are deduped, the commit button stays disabled until staging finishes, and stale draft fetches no longer fire after a version is deleted. A large round of dead canvas UI was removed along the way, which makes the editor lighter and easier to reason about.

This release also significantly expands the App catalog with six new prebuilt apps, adds Cloud Storage and EC2 load balancer support, polishes the Console dashboard, and tightens how Agents handle drafts, tokens, and integration resources.

### App install and catalog

The App install flow is more flexible and the catalog is much bigger. Install params now substitute cleanly into seed files, integration-resource params can use the resource name as the stored value, and the catalog now renders Lucide icons.

Six new prebuilt apps shipped to the manifest:

- **OSS Guard** — license and dependency policy guard for open source workflows.
- **TLS Monitor** — certificate expiration monitoring.
- **Semantic Pull Requests** — enforce conventional PR titles.
- **Cluster Autoscaler** — DigitalOcean + Grafana + Discord pipeline for cluster scaling.
- **PR Docs Review** — automated docs review on pull requests.
- **Auto Merge Bot** — policy-driven PR auto-merging.

The Coolify Watcher app was also added to the manifest, and the catalog was reordered by category.

### New components and integration expansion

Cloudsmith support grew substantially with `onPackageCreated` and `onSecurityScanCompleted` triggers and new `resyncPackage`, `tagPackage`, and `deletePackage` actions.

AWS EC2 gained `createLoadbalancer` and `deleteLoadBalancer` components, and `createInstance` was expanded with more launch options. GCP added a full set of Cloud Storage bucket components, and existing GCP component icons were refreshed.

GitHub triggers now cover all pull request webhook actions, removing the previous allow-list and unblocking less common PR event flows.

### Agents

Agents picked up two new building blocks for working with Apps:

- A `list_resources` action so agents can enumerate integration-backed resources before configuring nodes.
- Patch-style draft updates, which let agents apply targeted node and edge edits instead of resending whole canvas YAML.

Agent token usage tracking was tightened end to end: stable usage IDs, sync before publishing tokens, managed-agent session usage tracking, and non-blocking stream completion when usage writes are slow. Agent tool descriptions are now capped to stay within provider limits.

### Console and Canvas polish

Console dashboards got a visual pass — refreshed panels and a cleaner chart palette. The Table widget link field now supports CEL, the node widget panel got a round of fixes, and the canvas header stays above the runs sidebar and notes stay visible while inspecting runs. Memory variables are now available in autocomplete suggestions.

### Workflow execution fixes

A pile of execution edge cases were closed out: 5-field cron ranges now validate correctly, multiline runner setup commands are preserved, stale execution events no longer downgrade finished nodes, webhook-finalized executions publish their final state, `previous()` now resolves the latest upstream event, missing execution node references resolve as nil, and pending requests against deleted nodes are processed cleanly. Tasks now respect a configurable SuperPlane webhook payload size limit.

### Removals and cleanup

Core `sendEmail` notifications and the email invitation API were removed. A large amount of dead canvas plumbing was deleted: legacy YAML modal, unsaved-state and manual save UI, save activity log, changeset apply/validate endpoints, legacy view modes, and unused UI props and components. `RunSpan` was replaced with defer-style spans, and a new `grpcerror` package centralizes gRPC error handling.

### Bug Fixes

- Hide signup admin setting without waitlist config ([#5590](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5590)) by @forestileao
- Submit waitlist emails with native form ([#5591](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5591)) by @forestileao
- Update preview-env-digitalocean manifest entry ([#5601](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5601)) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Update preview-env-github-aws manifest entry ([#5602](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5602)) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Avoid DescribeCanvas requests in canvas edit mode ([#5629](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5629)) by @mchalapuk
- Remove core sendEmail notifications ([#5635](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5635)) by @forestileao
- Remove email invitation API ([#5638](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5638)) by @forestileao
- Add magic code email worker metrics ([#5637](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5637)) by @forestileao
- Keep canvas header above runs sidebar ([#5642](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5642)) by @forestileao

records.changelog:
## What's new since v0.26.0

v0.27.0 doubles down on the Git-first App workflow introduced in v0.26.0. The stage, commit, and publish loop is now noticeably smoother — duplicate draft requests are deduped, the commit button stays disabled until staging finishes, and stale draft fetches no longer fire after a version is deleted. A large round of dead canvas UI was removed along the way, which makes the editor lighter and easier to reason about.

This release also significantly expands the App catalog with six new prebuilt apps, adds Cloud Storage and EC2 load balancer support, polishes the Console dashboard, and tightens how Agents handle drafts, tokens, and integration resources.

### App install and catalog

The App install flow is more flexible and the catalog is much bigger. Install params now substitute cleanly into seed files, integration-resource params can use the resource name as the stored value, and the catalog now renders Lucide icons.

Six new prebuilt apps shipped to the manifest:

- **OSS Guard** — license and dependency policy guard for open source workflows.
- **TLS Monitor** — certificate expiration monitoring.
- **Semantic Pull Requests** — enforce conventional PR titles.
- **Cluster Autoscaler** — DigitalOcean + Grafana + Discord pipeline for cluster scaling.
- **PR Docs Review** — automated docs review on pull requests.
- **Auto Merge Bot** — policy-driven PR auto-merging.

The Coolify Watcher app was also added to the manifest, and the catalog was reordered by category.

### New components and integration expansion

Cloudsmith support grew substantially with `onPackageCreated` and `onSecurityScanCompleted` triggers and new `resyncPackage`, `tagPackage`, and `deletePackage` actions.

AWS EC2 gained `createLoadbalancer` and `deleteLoadBalancer` components, and `createInstance` was expanded with more launch options. GCP added a full set of Cloud Storage bucket components, and existing GCP component icons were refreshed.

GitHub triggers now cover all pull request webhook actions, removing the previous allow-list and unblocking less common PR event flows.

### Agents

Agents picked up two new building blocks for working with Apps:

- A `list_resources` action so agents can enumerate integration-backed resources before configuring nodes.
- Patch-style draft updates, which let agents apply targeted node and edge edits instead of resending whole canvas YAML.

Agent token usage tracking was tightened end to end: stable usage IDs, sync before publishing tokens, managed-agent session usage tracking, and non-blocking stream completion when usage writes are slow. Agent tool descriptions are now capped to stay within provider limits.

### Console and Canvas polish

Console dashboards got a visual pass — refreshed panels and a cleaner chart palette. The Table widget link field now supports CEL, the node widget panel got a round of fixes, and the canvas header stays above the runs sidebar and notes stay visible while inspecting runs. Memory variables are now available in autocomplete suggestions.

### Workflow execution fixes

A pile of execution edge cases were closed out: 5-field cron ranges now validate correctly, multiline runner setup commands are preserved, stale execution events no longer downgrade finished nodes, webhook-finalized executions publish their final state, `previous()` now resolves the latest upstream event, missing execution node references resolve as nil, and pending requests against deleted nodes are processed cleanly. Tasks now respect a configurable SuperPlane webhook payload size limit.

### Removals and cleanup

Core `sendEmail` notifications and the email invitation API were removed. A large amount of dead canvas plumbing was deleted: legacy YAML modal, unsaved-state and manual save UI, save activity log, changeset apply/validate endpoints, legacy view modes, and unused UI props and components. `RunSpan` was replaced with defer-style spans, and a new `grpcerror` package centralizes gRPC error handling.

- Submit waitlist emails with native form ([#5591](https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5591)) by @forestileao

values.changelog:
## What's new since v0.26.0

v0.27.0 is a catalog and integration release. The SuperPlane App catalog grows with seven new pre-built apps, Cloudsmith jumps from a base integration to a full one with new triggers and lifecycle actions, and AWS, GCP, and GitHub each pick up new components. It also lands a long list of polish fixes for the draft, commit, and publish flow introduced in v0.26.0, and quietly retires a lot of legacy canvas plumbing.

### App catalog and install experience

The catalog now ships with several new ready-to-install apps:

- OSS Guard
- TLS Monitor
- Semantic Pull Requests
- Coolify Watcher
- Cluster Autoscaler (DigitalOcean + Grafana + Discord)
- PR Docs Review
- Auto Merge Bot

The DigitalOcean and GitHub + AWS preview-environment manifests were refreshed, and the manifest itself was reordered by category for easier browsing.

The install wizard added in v0.26.0 is more capable too. Install params are now substituted into seed files when an app is created, integration-resource fields support a `useNameAsValue` option for providers that key on names rather than IDs, and the app catalog can render Lucide icons alongside the existing icon sets.

### Cloudsmith

Cloudsmith went from a base integration to a fully-featured one:

- New triggers: `onPackageCreated` and `onSecurityScanCompleted`.
- New actions: `resyncPackage`, `tagPackage`, and `deletePackage`, on top of the `getPackage` shipped in v0.26.0.

This makes it possible to drive package promotion, retagging, security follow-up, and cleanup directly from SuperPlane workflows.

### Other integration and component additions

- **AWS EC2** gained `createLoadBalancer` and `deleteLoadBalancer` components, and the existing `createInstance` action exposes more of EC2's launch options.
- **GCP Cloud Storage** is now first-class, with new bucket components, and existing GCP component icons were refreshed.
- **GitHub** webhook triggers now cover the full set of pull request actions, so workflows can react to every state change emitted by the GitHub webhook.
- Memory variables show up in expression autocomplete, making persisted state easier to discover and reference.
- Tasks accept a configurable **webhook payload size limit**, so larger inbound payloads can be wired through without hitting a hard cap.

### Agents

Two new agent tools tighten the edit and discovery loop:

- `patch-style draft updates` let agents apply small graph edits to an app draft without rewriting the whole canvas YAML.
- A new `list resources` action lets agents enumerate provider-backed integration resources (repositories, projects, workspaces, etc.) the same way the UI does.

Agent tool descriptions are now trimmed to fit Anthropic's provider-side limit, so registering many tools no longer fails the session.

### Console and node widgets

The Console dashboard panels and chart palette were polished as part of the ongoing Console rollout. Table Link cells now resolve CEL expressions, matching the rest of the Table widget, and a handful of node widget paint problems were ironed out.

### Drafts, canvas, and execution reliability

This release closes a long list of edge cases in the draft, commit, and publish flow introduced in v0.26.0:

- Publish now only triggers on clean drafts and no longer silently auto-commits staged changes.
- The versions sidebar dedupes draft creation and staging requests, and the commit button stays disabled until the staging action fully completes.
- Canvas edit mode no longer issues `DescribeCanvas` requests it does not need, and stale `canvas_version_*` invalidations from the same tab are ignored.
- A new `canvas_version_deleted` event stops stale draft fetches after a draft is removed, and notes stay visible while viewing canvas runs.
- `CanvasNodeRequest` rows are only locked when still pending, and pending requests for deleted nodes are processed instead of getting stuck.

On the execution side, expression handling, event ordering, and cron parsing all got safer:

- Missing expression tokens resolve to `null` instead of raising, and missing execution node references resolve to nil.
- `previous()` now resolves to the latest upstream event.
- Stale execution events can no longer downgrade a node that already finished, and webhook-finalized executions reliably publish their final state.
- 5-field cron expressions are validated with the correct field ranges, fixing a long-standing parser bug.
- Multiline runner setup commands round-trip without losing line breaks.

### Platform cleanup, toolchain, and observability

The frontend toolchain moved to Vite 8 and Storybook 10.4.6. On the backend, `RunSpan` was retired in favor of defer-style spans, a new `grpcerror` package centralizes gRPC error handling, and a long tail of dead canvas plumbing (changesets, view modes, save/unsaved-state UI, the legacy YAML modal, `ExecutionChainPage`) was removed.

Observability got a few quieter improvements: webhook workers and the magic code email worker emit dedicated metrics, expected org auth failures are no longer reported as span errors, Sentry CDN production builds emit sourcemaps, and Dash0 export noise is filtered out of Sentry.

### Release engineering

SuperPlane's own release pipeline now generates a changelog and tags releases automatically — the pipeline used to produce these notes.

### Bug Fixes

- Hide signup admin setting without waitlist config (#5590) by @forestileao
- Submit waitlist emails with native form (#5591) by @forestileao
- Update preview-env-digitalocean manifest entry (#5601) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Update preview-env-github-aws manifest entry (#5602) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1
- Avoid DescribeCanvas requests in canvas edit mode (#5629) by @mchalapuk
- Remove core sendEmail notifications (#5635) by @forestileao
- Remove email invitation API (#5638) by @forestileao
- Add magic code email worker metrics (#5637) by @forestileao
- Keep canvas header above runs sidebar (#5642) by @forestileao
- Publish only clean drafts without auto-committing (#5632) by @lucaspin
- Only lock CanvasNodeRequest for processing when still pending (#5622) by @lucaspin
- Node Widget Improvements (#5640) by @andrecalil
- Simplify usage settings limits display (#5654) by @forestileao
- Publish agent token usage events (#5655) by @forestileao
- Preserve usage setup precondition errors (#5661) by @forestileao
- Portal rubric plan modal (#5668) by @forestileao
- Sync usage before publishing agent tokens (#5669) by @forestileao
- Track managed agent session usage (#5671) by @forestileao
- Avoid blocking agent stream completion on usage (#5672) by @forestileao
- Always refetch usage settings page (#5673) by @forestileao
- Ignore version creation invalidations on same tab (#5680) by @lucaspin
- Emit canvas_version_deleted and stop stale draft fetches after delete (#5686) by @lucaspin
- Suppress duplicate draft creation requests from versions sidebar (#5690) by @lucaspin
- Dedupe commit and publish draft staging API requests (#5692) by @lucaspin
- Keep commit button disabled until staging action fully completes (#5693) by @lucaspin
- Preserve multiline runner setup commands (#5695) by @forestileao
- Stop stale execution events downgrading finished nodes (#5696) by @forestileao
- Publish execution state after webhook-finalized executions (#5697) by @forestileao
- Keep notes visible while viewing canvas runs (#5699) by @shiroyasha
- Make previous resolve latest upstream event (#5702) by @forestileao

- Harden release image build prerequisites (#5698) by @forestileao
- Use ctx when listing integrations, service accounts, and invite link (#5735) by @lucaspin

- Stop marking expected org auth failures as span errors (#5764) by @shiroyasha
- Validate 5-field cron ranges correctly (#4825) by @immanuwell

### Improvements

- Remove dead canvas UI plumbing and unused helpers (#5665) by @lucaspin
- Remove unused canvas UI props and dead components (#5666) by @lucaspin
- Remove legacy canvas view modes and stale header modes (#5667) by @lucaspin
- Introduce grpcerror package (#5677) by @shiroyasha

### New features

- Add cloudsmith onPackageCreated and onSecurityScanCompleted triggers (#5562) by @WashingtonKK
- App Installation Improvements (#5584) by @andrecalil
- Substitute install params in seed files (#5597) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1

- Add OSS Guard to app manifest (#5604) by @bender-rodriguez-unit1

configuration.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

retry() {
  local attempt=1
  local max_attempts=3
  local delay=20

  while true; do
    if "$@"; then
      return 0
    fi

    local exit_code=$?
    if [ "$attempt" -ge "$max_attempts" ]; then
      return "$exit_code"
    fi

    echo "Command failed with exit code ${exit_code}; retrying in ${delay}s (${attempt}/${max_attempts}): $*" >&2
    sleep "$delay"
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    delay=$((delay * 2))
  done
}

IMAGE="${ECR_REGISTRY}/${ECR_REPO}:${COMMIT_SHA}"
VERSION_TAG="${ECR_REGISTRY}/${ECR_REPO}:${REL_VERSION}"
ARCH_IMAGE="${IMAGE}-amd64"

WORKDIR=$(mktemp -d)
GIT_ASKPASS="$WORKDIR/git-askpass.sh"
cat > "$GIT_ASKPASS" <<'ASKPASS'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
case "$1" in
  Username*) printf '%s' 'x-access-token' ;;
  Password*) printf '%s' "$GITHUB_TOKEN" ;;
esac
ASKPASS
chmod 700 "$GIT_ASKPASS"
GIT_ASKPASS="$GIT_ASKPASS" git clone "https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane.git" "$WORKDIR/src"
cd "$WORKDIR/src"
git checkout "$COMMIT_SHA"
git fetch --depth=1 origin main
git checkout FETCH_HEAD -- release/lib/image-build-prerequisites.sh release/superplane-image/build.sh release/superplane-demo-image/build.sh

aws ecr get-login-password --region "$AWS_REGION" \
  | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin "$ECR_REGISTRY"

export DEMO_IMAGE_REPO="${ECR_REGISTRY}/${ECR_REPO}"
retry bash release/superplane-demo-image/build.sh "$COMMIT_SHA" amd64
retry docker buildx imagetools create -t "$IMAGE" "$ARCH_IMAGE"
retry docker buildx imagetools create -t "$VERSION_TAG" "$ARCH_IMAGE"

cat > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE" <<JSON
{"image":"$IMAGE","versionTag":"$VERSION_TAG","commit":"$COMMIT_SHA","version":"$REL_VERSION"}
JSON
configuration.setup_commands:
command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 && dpkg -s ca-certificates >/dev/null 2>&1 || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates)
command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends docker.io docker-compose-plugin && systemctl enable --now docker)
docker compose version >/dev/null 2>&1 || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends docker-compose-plugin)
docker buildx version >/dev/null 2>&1 || (mkdir -p ~/.docker/cli-plugins && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/docker/buildx/releases/download/v0.19.3/buildx-v0.19.3.linux-amd64" -o ~/.docker/cli-plugins/docker-buildx && chmod +x ~/.docker/cli-plugins/docker-buildx)
command -v aws >/dev/null 2>&1 || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl unzip ca-certificates && curl -sSL "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-x86_64.zip" -o /tmp/awscli.zip && unzip -q /tmp/awscli.zip -d /tmp && /tmp/aws/install)
command -v make >/dev/null 2>&1 || (apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential)

configuration.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

THRESHOLD=$(date -u -d "${TTL_HOURS} hours ago" --iso-8601=seconds)
echo "Sweeping canary EC2s launched before $THRESHOLD in $AWS_REGION"

INSTANCES=$(aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --region "$AWS_REGION" \
  --filters "Name=tag:purpose,Values=release-canary" "Name=instance-state-name,Values=running,stopped,pending" \
  --query "Reservations[].Instances[?LaunchTime<\`$THRESHOLD\`].InstanceId" \
  --output text)

if [ -z "$INSTANCES" ] || [ "$INSTANCES" = "None" ]; then
  echo "No stale canary instances to terminate."
  cat > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE" <<JSON
{"terminated": [], "threshold": "$THRESHOLD"}
JSON
  exit 0
fi

echo "Terminating: $INSTANCES"
aws ec2 terminate-instances --region "$AWS_REGION" --instance-ids $INSTANCES

TERMINATED_JSON=$(printf '%s\n' $INSTANCES | jq -R . | jq -s .)
cat > "$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE" <<JSON
{"terminated": $TERMINATED_JSON, "threshold": "$THRESHOLD"}
JSON
configuration.setup_commands:
